name: Australia
shortDescription: World-class education with up to 4 years post-study work visa.
description: Australia is one of the top study destinations for Bangladeshi students, known for high-quality universities, multicultural environment, and strong post-graduation work opportunities.

meta.seoTitle: Study in Australia from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education
meta.seoDescription: Explore top universities, courses, scholarships, and visa guidance for studying in Australia.

highlights.title: World-Class Universities
highlights.description: Home to several of the world's top-ranked universities.

highlights.title: Post-Study Work Visa
highlights.description: Up to 4 years of post-study work opportunities.

highlights.title: Multicultural Society
highlights.description: A diverse and welcoming environment for international students.

highlights.title: Beautiful Scenery
highlights.description: Enjoy stunning landscapes and vibrant city life.

workRights.student: 48 hours per fortnight during academic sessions, full-time during breaks.
workRights.afterGraduation: Post-Study Work stream of the Temporary Graduate visa (subclass 485) allows for 2-4 years of work.

visaRequirements: Academic transcripts and work experience documents

faq.question: What are the general entry requirements for Australian universities?
faq.answer: Typically, you need to have completed secondary education, meet English language proficiency requirements (IELTS, PTE), and have sufficient funds for tuition and living expenses.

faq.question: Can I work while studying in Australia?
faq.answer: Yes, student visa holders are generally allowed to work up to 48 hours per fortnight during study periods and full-time during scheduled course breaks.

faq.question: What is the post-study work visa option in Australia?
faq.answer: The Temporary Graduate visa (subclass 485) offers a Post-Study Work stream, allowing eligible graduates to stay and work for 2 to 4 years, depending on their qualification and regional study.

faq.question: Is health insurance mandatory for international students?
faq.answer: Yes, it is compulsory for international students to have Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) for the entire duration of their stay.

name: Canada
shortDescription: Welcoming country with excellent PR pathways.
description: Canada offers high-quality education, affordable living in many provinces, and great post-graduation work permit options for Bangladeshi students.

meta.seoTitle: Study in Canada from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education
meta.seoDescription: Best universities, courses, and immigration pathways in Canada for Bangladeshi students.

highlights.title: High-Quality Education
highlights.description: Globally recognized for its excellent academic standards.

highlights.title: PR Pathways
highlights.description: Clear and accessible pathways to permanent residency.

highlights.title: Affordable Living
highlights.description: Lower cost of living compared to other top study destinations.

highlights.title: Safe and Inclusive
highlights.description: Known for its safety, diversity, and welcoming culture.

workRights.student: Up to 20 hours per week during school terms and full-time during scheduled breaks.
workRights.afterGraduation: Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) allows for up to 3 years of work experience.

visaRequirements: Proof of financial support (Guaranteed Investment Certificate - GIC)

faq.question: What are the common English proficiency tests accepted in Canada?
faq.answer: IELTS Academic is the most commonly accepted test. Some institutions might also accept TOEFL, PTE, or Duolingo.

faq.question: Can I get permanent residency in Canada after studying?
faq.answer: Yes, Canada offers several pathways to permanent residency for international graduates, notably through the Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) and various Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

faq.question: Is a GIC (Guaranteed Investment Certificate) required for a Canadian study permit?
faq.answer: While not always strictly mandatory, a GIC is highly recommended and often crucial for demonstrating sufficient funds, especially for students applying through the Student Direct Stream (SDS).

faq.question: What is the average tuition fee for international students in Canada?
faq.answer: Tuition fees vary significantly by program and institution, typically ranging from CAD 15,000 to CAD 35,000 per year for undergraduate programs, and CAD 10,000 to CAD 25,000 for graduate programs.

name: United Kingdom
description: The UK is a favorite destination with prestigious universities, shorter course durations, and excellent career prospects.

meta.seoTitle: Study in UK from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education
meta.seoDescription: Top UK universities, courses, and student visa guidance.

highlights.title: Prestigious Universities
highlights.description: Home to some of the oldest and most respected universities.

highlights.title: Shorter Courses
highlights.description: Complete your Master's in one year.

highlights.title: Graduate Route Visa
highlights.description: Stay and work in the UK for up to 2 years after graduation.

highlights.title: Rich History and Culture
highlights.description: Explore a country with a deep and influential history.

workRights.student: Up to 20 hours per week during term time and full-time during holidays.
workRights.afterGraduation: Graduate Route visa allows for 2 years of work (3 years for PhD graduates).

visaRequirements: Academic Technology Approval Scheme (ATAS) certificate (if applicable)

faq.question: What is a CAS (Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies)?
faq.answer: A CAS is a unique reference number issued by your university that you need to apply for a Student visa. It confirms that you have been accepted onto a course.

faq.question: How much funds do I need to show for a UK student visa?
faq.answer: You need to show that you have enough money to cover your course fees for one academic year (up to a maximum of £11,385) and your living costs (£1,334 per month for up to 9 months in London, or £1,023 outside London).

faq.question: Can my dependents join me in the UK?
faq.answer: Only postgraduate students undertaking courses of 9 months or longer, or government-sponsored students, can bring dependents. This rule is subject to change.

faq.question: What is the Graduate Route visa?
faq.answer: The Graduate Route allows international students who have successfully completed a degree at a UK Higher Education Provider to stay and work, or look for work, for 2 years (or 3 years for PhD graduates).

name: United States
shortDescription: Home to world-renowned universities and STEM opportunities.
description: The USA provides unparalleled academic excellence, research opportunities, and OPT extension for STEM graduates.

meta.seoTitle: Study in USA from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education
meta.seoDescription: Study in top American universities with scholarships and visa support.

highlights.title: Top-Ranked Universities
highlights.description: Leading institutions for research and innovation.

highlights.title: STEM Opportunities
highlights.description: Excellent prospects for students in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math.

highlights.title: Flexible Education
highlights.description: A wide variety of courses and flexible curriculum.

highlights.title: Cultural Diversity
highlights.description: Experience a melting pot of cultures and ideas.

workRights.student: Up to 20 hours per week on-campus. Off-campus work requires special authorization.
workRights.afterGraduation: Optional Practical Training (OPT) for up to 12 months, with a 24-month extension for STEM fields.

visaRequirements: Form I-20 from a SEVP-approved school
Payment of the SEVIS I-901 fee
Visa interview at a U.S. embassy or consulate

faq.question: What is the F-1 visa and who needs it?
faq.answer: The F-1 visa is for international students who wish to pursue academic studies in the United States. All non-immigrant international students need this visa to study at a U.S. institution.

faq.question: What is OPT and how does it work?
faq.answer: Optional Practical Training (OPT) allows F-1 students to work in their field of study for up to 12 months after graduation. STEM graduates may be eligible for a 24-month extension.

faq.question: Are there scholarships available for international students in the USA?
faq.answer: Yes, many universities offer merit-based or need-based scholarships. External organizations and government programs also provide funding opportunities. Research early for best chances.

faq.question: Can my family accompany me on an F-1 visa?
faq.answer: Spouses and unmarried minor children (under 21 years old) of F-1 visa holders can apply for an F-2 visa to accompany them, but F-2 visa holders cannot work.

name: New Zealand
shortDescription: Safe and beautiful country with quality education.
description: New Zealand offers a safe environment, outstanding education system, and good post-study work rights.

meta.seoTitle: Study in New Zealand from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education
meta.seoDescription: Quality education and peaceful lifestyle in New Zealand.

highlights.title: Safe and Peaceful
highlights.description: Consistently ranked as one of the safest countries in the world.

highlights.title: Stunning Nature
highlights.description: Breathtaking landscapes and outdoor activities.

highlights.title: Quality of Life
highlights.description: Enjoy a high standard of living and work-life balance.

highlights.title: Post-Study Work Rights
highlights.description: Opportunities to work after graduation.

workRights.student: Up to 20 hours per week during the academic year and full-time during holidays.
workRights.afterGraduation: Post-study work visa for 1-3 years, depending on the qualification.

visaRequirements: Offer of Place from an approved education provider
Proof of sufficient funds to live and study

faq.question: What is an 'Offer of Place' in New Zealand?
faq.answer: An Offer of Place is an official letter from a New Zealand education provider confirming that they have accepted you onto a course. It's a mandatory document for your visa application.

faq.question: How much money do I need to show for a New Zealand student visa?
faq.answer: You generally need to show you have NZ$20,000 for each year of study to cover your living costs. If your course is less than a year, you need NZ$1,667 for each month.

faq.question: Can I work in New Zealand after graduation?
faq.answer: Yes, eligible graduates can apply for a Post-Study Work Visa, which allows them to stay and work in New Zealand for 1 to 3 years, depending on their qualification.

faq.question: Are there age limits for studying in New Zealand?
faq.answer: While there isn't a strict upper age limit, applicants under 18 or over 30 might face additional scrutiny or requirements, especially if their study plans seem unusual for their age.

name: Denmark
shortDescription: Innovative education with focus on sustainability.
description: Denmark is known for its progressive education system, research opportunities, and high quality of life.

meta.seoTitle: Study in Denmark from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education
meta.seoDescription: Innovative programs and scholarships in Denmark.

highlights.title: Focus on Innovation
highlights.description: A leader in design, technology, and sustainable solutions.

highlights.title: High Quality of Life
highlights.description: One of the happiest countries in the world.

highlights.title: English Proficiency
highlights.description: Widely spoken, making it easy for international students to adapt.

highlights.title: Green and Sustainable
highlights.description: A strong commitment to environmental and social responsibility.

workRights.student: Up to 20 hours per week during the school year and full-time during holidays.
workRights.afterGraduation: An Establishment Card allows graduates to stay for up to 2 years to find work.

visaRequirements: Proof of admission to a Danish higher education institution
Proof of sufficient funds (approx. DKK 6,321 per month)

faq.question: Are there tuition fees for international students in Denmark?
faq.answer: Students from EU/EEA countries do not pay tuition fees. For non-EU/EEA students, tuition fees apply and vary by program and institution.

faq.question: What is an 'Establishment Card'?
faq.answer: The Establishment Card is a residence permit for international graduates from Danish higher education programs, allowing them to stay in Denmark for up to 2 years to seek employment.

faq.question: Is Danish language proficiency required to study?
faq.answer: No, many programs are taught entirely in English. However, learning some Danish can greatly enhance your social life and job prospects.

faq.question: What kind of accommodation is available for students?
faq.answer: Student dormitories, private rentals, and shared apartments are common. It's advisable to secure accommodation before arriving, especially in larger cities like Copenhagen.

name: Sweden
shortDescription: Global leader in innovation and technology.
description: Sweden offers world-class education with a strong emphasis on creativity, sustainability, and research.

meta.seoTitle: Study in Sweden from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education

highlights.title: Innovation Hub
highlights.description: Birthplace of many successful startups and tech companies.

highlights.title: Emphasis on Creativity
highlights.description: Education system that encourages independent and creative thinking.

highlights.title: Social Equality
highlights.description: A society that values equality and openness.

highlights.title: Natural Beauty
highlights.description: Vast forests, numerous lakes, and stunning coastlines.

workRights.student: No official limit on work hours, but studies must be the primary focus.
workRights.afterGraduation: Can apply for a residence permit to seek employment or start a business for up to 12 months.

visaRequirements: Letter of acceptance from a Swedish university
Proof of funds to support yourself (SEK 8,568 per month)

faq.question: Are tuition fees charged in Sweden for international students?
faq.answer: Students from EU/EEA countries are exempt from tuition fees. Non-EU/EEA students are required to pay tuition fees, which vary by program and university.

faq.question: Can I work while studying in Sweden?
faq.answer: There is no official limit to the number of hours international students can work in Sweden, but your studies must remain your primary focus.

faq.question: What are the post-study options in Sweden?
faq.answer: After completing your studies, you can apply for a residence permit to seek employment or start a business in Sweden for up to 12 months.

faq.question: Is Swedish language proficiency required for admission?
faq.answer: No, many Bachelor's and Master's programs are taught in English. However, learning Swedish can be beneficial for daily life and career opportunities.

faq.question: Are Finnish universities free for international students?
faq.answer: No, tuition fees apply to non-EU/EEA students. Scholarships are available to help cover tuition costs.

faq.question: Can I work while studying in Finland?
faq.answer: Yes, student residence permit holders can work up to 25 hours per week during term time and full-time during holidays.

faq.question: What are the post-study work options in Finland?
faq.answer: Graduates can apply for a 2-year post-study permit to search for employment or establish a business in Finland.

faq.question: Do I need to learn Finnish to study there?
faq.answer: While many programs are in English, basic Finnish can be helpful for daily life and cultural integration. Some universities offer Finnish language courses.

shortDescription: Multicultural hub in the heart of Europe.

faq.question: What are the main languages spoken in Belgium?
faq.answer: Belgium has three official languages: Dutch, French, and German. English is also widely spoken, especially in academic and international settings.

faq.question: Can I work part-time while studying in Belgium?
faq.answer: Yes, international students are allowed to work up to 20 hours per week during term time and full-time during official holiday periods, provided they have a valid residence permit.

faq.question: Are there opportunities to stay in Belgium after graduation?
faq.answer: Graduates can apply for an 'orientation year' residence permit, which allows them up to 12 months to seek employment or establish a business in Belgium.

faq.question: What is the cost of tuition for international students in Belgium?
faq.answer: Tuition fees are relatively affordable compared to other Western European countries, ranging from €900 to €4,000 per year, though some specialized programs may be higher.

faq.question: Is English widely spoken in Malta?
faq.answer: Yes, English is one of Malta's official languages, and it's widely spoken across the island, making it easy for international students to communicate and study.

faq.question: Can I work while studying in Malta?
faq.answer: International students can work up to 20 hours per week after the first 90 days of their study program, provided they obtain a valid work permit.

faq.question: What is the average cost of tuition in Malta?
faq.answer: Tuition fees vary significantly depending on the institution and program but generally range from €2,000 to €10,000 per year for non-EU/EEA students.

faq.question: What type of accommodation is available for students?
faq.answer: Students can choose from university residences, private rented apartments, or shared accommodation, often at reasonable prices, especially outside peak tourist seasons.

visaRequirements: Acceptance letter from a Hungarian university

faq.question: Is Hungarian language knowledge required for studying?
faq.answer: No, many programs are offered entirely in English, particularly at the Master's and PhD levels. However, basic Hungarian can be beneficial for daily life.

faq.question: What is the post-study visa option in Hungary?
faq.answer: Graduates can apply for a 'Study-to-Work' visa, allowing them to stay in Hungary for up to 9 months after completing their studies to seek employment.

faq.question: How much does it cost to study in Hungary?
faq.answer: Hungary is considered one of the most affordable countries in Europe for international students, with tuition fees ranging from €2,500 to €12,000 per year depending on the program.

faq.question: Can I work part-time while on a student visa in Hungary?
faq.answer: Yes, international students holding a valid residence permit for study purposes are allowed to work up to 24 hours per week during study periods and 90 days or 66 working days outside the study period.

faq.question: Are degrees from Cyprus recognized internationally?
faq.answer: Yes, degrees from accredited Cypriot universities are recognized across the EU and internationally, ensuring good prospects for further education or employment.

faq.question: Can international students work in Cyprus?
faq.answer: International students are permitted to work up to 20 hours per week in specific sectors like petrol stations, agriculture, and hospitality after completing six months of their studies.

faq.question: What is the cost of tuition in Cyprus for international students?
faq.answer: Tuition fees are generally competitive, ranging from €2,500 to €8,000 per year for Bachelor's programs and €5,000 to €10,000 for Master's programs.

faq.question: Is it safe to live and study in Cyprus?
faq.answer: Cyprus is known for its very low crime rate and safe environment, making it a popular choice for international students seeking a secure place to live and study.

name: Ireland
description: Ireland is home to many global tech companies and offers excellent post-study work opportunities.

meta.seoTitle: Study in Ireland from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education
meta.seoDescription: Study in Ireland - Europe's growing tech destination.

highlights.title: European Tech Hub
highlights.description: Headquarters for many multinational tech companies.

highlights.title: Friendly and Welcoming
highlights.description: Known for its hospitality and friendly people.

highlights.title: Post-Study Work Visa
highlights.description: Stay and work in Ireland for up to 2 years.

highlights.title: Rich Literary Heritage
highlights.description: The land of Joyce, Yeats, and Wilde.

workRights.student: Up to 20 hours per week during term and 40 hours during holidays.
workRights.afterGraduation: Third Level Graduate Scheme allows for a 1-2 year stay to seek employment.

visaRequirements: Letter of Acceptance from an Irish university

faq.question: What is the Third Level Graduate Scheme in Ireland?
faq.answer: This scheme allows non-EU/EEA graduates to remain in Ireland for a period after their studies to seek employment. The duration depends on the level of qualification, typically 12 or 24 months.

faq.question: Is it expensive to live in Ireland as a student?
faq.answer: While cities like Dublin can be costly, Ireland offers a good quality of life. Student budgets typically range from €7,000 to €12,000 per year, excluding tuition fees.

faq.question: Are there many job opportunities in Ireland after graduation?
faq.answer: Ireland, particularly Dublin, is a major hub for technology, pharmaceuticals, and finance, offering significant job opportunities for graduates, especially those with in-demand skills.

faq.question: What kind of student accommodation is available?
faq.answer: Options include on-campus student residences, purpose-built student accommodation (PBSA), private rented apartments, and shared housing. Booking in advance is highly recommended.

name: Malaysia
description: Malaysia is a popular choice for Bangladeshi students due to low cost, English-taught programs, and close cultural ties.

meta.seoTitle: Study in Malaysia from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education
meta.seoDescription: Affordable study in Malaysia with many Bangladeshi students.

highlights.title: Affordable Tuition Fees
highlights.description: High-quality education at a fraction of the cost.

highlights.title: Cultural Similarities
highlights.description: A comfortable and familiar environment for Bangladeshi students.

highlights.title: English-Taught Programs
highlights.description: A wide range of courses are taught in English.

highlights.title: Delicious Food
highlights.description: A paradise for food lovers with diverse culinary options.

workRights.student: Up to 20 hours per week during semester breaks only.
workRights.afterGraduation: Generally not permitted; requires a separate employment pass.

visaRequirements: Offer letter from a Malaysian university

faq.question: What is the Visa Approval Letter (VAL) in Malaysia?
faq.answer: The VAL is a document issued by the Malaysian Immigration Department, approving your student visa application. It is a crucial step before you can enter Malaysia for studies.

faq.question: Can I work while studying in Malaysia?
faq.answer: International students are generally permitted to work part-time for up to 20 hours per week only during semester breaks or holidays of more than 7 days, in specific sectors.

faq.question: Is English the medium of instruction in Malaysian universities?
faq.answer: Yes, most private higher education institutions and many public universities in Malaysia offer programs taught entirely in English, especially at the postgraduate level.

faq.question: What are the post-study work opportunities in Malaysia?
faq.answer: Direct post-study work permits are generally not available. Graduates typically need to secure a job offer and have their employer apply for an employment pass on their behalf.

name: Netherlands
description: The Netherlands offers many English-taught programs and a very international student environment.

meta.seoTitle: Study in Netherlands from Bangladesh | Dreamway Global Education

highlights.title: International Environment
highlights.description: A large and diverse international student community.

highlights.title: High English Proficiency
highlights.description: The majority of the population speaks English.

highlights.title: Problem-Based Learning
highlights.description: An interactive and student-centered approach to education.

highlights.title: Cycling Culture
highlights.description: A healthy, affordable, and fun way to get around.

workRights.student: Up to 16 hours per week, or full-time during summer months.
workRights.afterGraduation: An orientation year permit allows graduates one year to find a job as a highly skilled migrant.

visaRequirements: Provisional acceptance letter from a Dutch university
Proof of sufficient funds (€900 per month)

faq.question: Is it easy to find English-taught programs in the Netherlands?
faq.answer: Yes, the Netherlands offers a wide range of English-taught Bachelor's and Master's programs, making it a very popular destination for international students.

faq.question: What is the 'orientation year' permit after graduation?
faq.answer: The 'zoekjaar' or orientation year permit allows non-EU/EEA graduates one year to stay in the Netherlands to find a job as a highly skilled migrant or start their own business.

faq.question: Do I need health insurance as an international student?
faq.answer: Yes, health insurance is mandatory. The type of insurance depends on your nationality and whether you plan to work alongside your studies.

faq.question: What is the best way to travel around Dutch cities?
faq.answer: Cycling is very popular and convenient in the Netherlands, with excellent cycling infrastructure. Public transport (trains, trams, buses) is also efficient and widely used.
